originalTitle: 'We want a voice in our land' - the people evicted to build Nigeria's capital
title: Big City, Big Promises: A Story from Nigeria!
content:
Hello, Friends! From Sunny Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter, here from the amazing continent of Africa! I have a very important story to share with you today from a super big country called Nigeria. It's a story about homes, promises, and a big, beautiful city. Let's learn together!

What is a Capital City?

Do you know what a "capital city" is? It's like the most important city in a country! It's where the country's leaders work and make big decisions for everyone. Imagine your school principal's office – that's a bit like a capital city for your school! Many important buildings are there, like big offices and special meeting places. Countries usually choose one special city to be their capital. *Did you know?* The capital city of Nigeria is called Abuja! It's a very famous city in Africa!

Meet Abuja: Nigeria's Special Capital

A long time ago, Nigeria needed a new, special capital city. It's a very, very big country with lots and lots of people! So, they decided to build a brand new city called Abuja. Imagine building a whole city from scratch! Like drawing a picture of a house and then actually building it! Abuja was planned to be a very modern and beautiful city, a place where everyone in Nigeria could feel proud. It has wide roads, tall buildings, and green parks. It's a wonderful place to visit!

Homes for a Big Dream: How Abuja Was Built

Now, when they decided to build Abuja, there were already people living on that land. Yes, just like you live in your house, many families had their homes there. They had their farms, their schools, and their communities. This land was their home for a very, very long time, even before Abuja was a dream. The government wanted to build a beautiful new capital, but what about the people already living there?

The People Who Lived There First

To build the new city, these people had to move. Imagine someone telling you that you need to leave your home so they can build something new. That would be very sad, right? So, the government told these families that they needed to move. This is called being 'evicted'. It means you are asked to leave your home. It's a big change for families. *Did you know?* Nigeria is the country with the most people in all of Africa! It has over 200 million people! That's a lot of friends!

Special Promises Made

When the government asked these families to move, they made some very important promises. A promise is like saying, "I will definitely do this for you!" The government promised to help these families. They said they would give them new homes in new places. They promised to build new schools for their children. They also said they would build new hospitals for them to stay healthy. And they promised to help them find new jobs or new land for farming. These were big, kind promises!

Waiting for Promises: Why the People Are Sad Now

Many years have passed since Abuja was built. The city is now big and bustling. But guess what? Many of the people who moved are still waiting for those promises to come true. They say that the new homes were never given to them. They say the new schools and hospitals were not built. And they are still struggling to find good jobs or new land to farm. They feel very sad and disappointed because the promises were not kept. *Did you know?* It is very important for people to have a safe home and food to eat. These are called basic needs!

What Do the People Want?

These brave people are now asking for their voices to be heard. They are saying, "Please, government, remember your promises!" They want new homes, new schools, and help to live good lives, just like they were promised. They want to be treated fairly. They feel like they have lost their homes and their way of life, and they just want the government to do what they said they would do. They want a voice in their land, meaning they want to be listened to.

Why This Story Matters

This story is important because it teaches us about keeping promises. When someone makes a promise, it's important to keep it, especially when it affects people's homes and lives. It also reminds us that everyone deserves a safe place to live and a chance to go to school and be healthy. Governments are there to help all the people in their country, and that means listening to everyone, even if they had to move for a big city dream.

Let's Think Together!

So, friends, let's remember the people who helped build Abuja. Let's hope that the government hears their voices and fulfills the promises made a long time ago. It's about being fair and kind to everyone. That's all for today from your African news reporter! See you next time!
summary: People who had to move from their homes so Nigeria could build its capital city, Abuja, are still waiting for the government to keep its promises of new homes, schools, and help to build new lives.
whyMatters: It's super important for governments to keep their promises to people, especially when it's about their homes and futures, so everyone is treated fairly and has what they need to live happy and safe.

originalTitle: Artemis II crew take 'spectacular' image of Earth
title: Wow! Astronauts Take a Picture of Earth from Space!
content:
Hello, amazing kids of Africa! Are you ready for some super exciting news from space? Guess what? Some brave astronauts are flying around the Moon right now, and they took a super-duper special picture of our beautiful Earth! It looks so cool from way, way up high!

Who are these Space Heroes?

Imagine being a superhero, but instead of flying around a city, you fly into space! That's what astronauts are! They are very smart and brave people who train for many years to go to space. One of these amazing space heroes is named Reid Wiseman. He is the boss of their special spaceship. He and his friends are part of a team called the 'Artemis II crew'. They wear special suits and eat special food in space! Their job is to explore and learn new things. Did you know? Astronauts are like super explorers who travel to places no one has been before! They are amazing scientists and pilots all rolled into one!

What is Artemis II?

So, why are these heroes going to space? They are on a big adventure called 'Artemis II'. It's not just a small trip; it's a huge step to learn more about the Moon. The 'Artemis' missions are all about helping us go back to the Moon and maybe even to other planets like Mars one day! This trip is important because it's helping us get ready for when astronauts will actually land on the Moon again. Did you know? The last time humans walked on the Moon was a very long time ago, even before your parents were born! It was in 1972!

The Orion Spaceship

These astronauts are travelling in a super cool spaceship called 'Orion'. Think of Orion like a tiny, very strong house that flies in space. It's built to keep the astronauts safe and sound as they zoom really fast towards the Moon. It has all the important things they need, like air to breathe, water to drink, and computers to help them fly. It's like a flying home for the astronauts in the big, dark space. Did you know? The Orion spaceship can travel faster than any car or airplane you know! It needs to be super speedy to reach the Moon, which is very far away!

The Amazing Photo!

Now, for the really exciting part! Commander Reid Wiseman looked out of a window in the Orion spaceship. And guess what he saw? Our beautiful Earth! He took a picture, a 'snap' as we call it. He said it was 'spectacular'! This means it was unbelievably wonderful and amazing. Can you imagine seeing our whole Earth from far, far away? It looks like a bright blue marble with swirling white clouds. Did you know? From space, Earth looks mostly blue because it has so much water, like the big oceans you see on a map! That's why it's called the 'Blue Planet'!

Our Beautiful Home, Earth

When you look at a picture of Earth from space, you can see big blue oceans and swirling white clouds. You can also see green land where trees and plants grow, and brown parts where there are mountains or deserts. It reminds us that our Earth is a very special place, full of life and beauty. It's our home, and it's the only home we have! Looking at it from space makes you feel how precious it is. Did you know? Earth is often called the "Blue Planet" because about 70% of its surface is covered in water! That's a lot of water!

Heading to the Moon!

The Artemis II crew are not landing on the Moon this time. They are flying *around* the Moon, going on a big loop before coming back home. This trip helps them test the Orion spaceship and see how everything works far away from Earth. It's like a practice run for bigger trips in the future. As they fly further, Earth gets smaller and smaller in their window, and the Moon gets bigger! Did you know? The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite, which means it's Earth's special friend that goes everywhere with it in space! It's always orbiting around us.

Why This Matters for All of Us

This amazing picture and this whole mission are super important. They show us that humans can do incredible things! It teaches us more about space, about our Earth, and about how we can explore new places. It inspires us to dream big and learn more about science and space. Maybe one day, one of you amazing kids will be an astronaut, flying to the Moon or even Mars! Did you know? Every time astronauts go to space, they learn new things that help us here on Earth, like making better medicines or improving our phones and computers!
summary: Brave astronauts on the Artemis II mission took a beautiful picture of Earth from their Orion spaceship as they flew towards the Moon.
whyMatters: This mission helps us learn more about space and get ready to send people back to the Moon, inspiring us all to dream big about exploring new worlds.

originalTitle: After 16 years in power, can Viktor Orban finally be unseated?
title: A Big Day Coming in Far-Away Hungary!
content:
Hello from Your Africa Reporter!

Hello, my amazing friends all over Africa! It's your friendly reporter here, bringing you super exciting news from around the world. Even though we live here in Africa, it's super cool to learn about what's happening in other places, far, far away. Today, we're going on an adventure to a country called Hungary!

What's Happening Far, Far Away?

Hungary is a country that is very, very far from us. It is in a place called Europe. Europe is another continent, just like Africa is a continent! Imagine drawing a big map; Africa is on one side, and Europe is on another. Hungary has its own people, its own schools, and its own special food, just like our countries. It’s a beautiful place with castles and rivers. It has its own songs and stories too. Did you know? Hungary is famous for a special kind of red spice called paprika! It makes food taste yummy and has a lovely red colour. Also, the capital city of Hungary is called Budapest. It has a big river called the Danube running through it!

Meet Mr. Viktor Orban

In Hungary, there is a very important person named Mr. Viktor Orban. He is like the head teacher of the whole country! We call him the leader or the Prime Minister. His job is to help make decisions for everyone in Hungary. He helps decide about schools, roads, and how people can live happily and safely. Being a leader is a very big job because you have to think about many, many things for all the people. He has been the main leader in Hungary for a very, very long time. Imagine if your head teacher stayed the head teacher for 16 whole years! That is a super long time, isn't it? Mr. Orban first became the leader a long, long time ago, and he has been the leader ever since. Did you know? In many countries, the main leader can be called different things. Sometimes they are called a President, like in many African countries. Sometimes they are called a Prime Minister, like Mr. Orban. But their job is similar: to lead the country!

How Long is 16 Years?

Let's think about 16 years. How old are you? Maybe you are 7 years old? Or 8? 16 years is much, much longer than that! It's like being your age, and then being that age again, and then a little bit more! That’s a lot of birthdays! If you started school when you were 5, 16 years means you would have finished primary school, then secondary school, and even gone to university to learn more! Wow, that is a really, really long time. For a leader to be in power for 16 years means they have been making big decisions for a very, very long time. Many children born 16 years ago in Hungary have grown up knowing only Mr. Orban as their country's main leader. It's a big part of their lives. Did you know? Some leaders in the world have been in power for even longer! It's interesting to see how different countries choose their leaders and for how long. It's a big choice for any country.

What is an Election Day?

Now, something very exciting is going to happen in Hungary very soon. It's called an election day! Have you ever chosen something with your friends? Like which game to play, or which story to read? An election is like that, but for everyone in the whole country. On election day, grown-up people in Hungary get to 'vote.' What is voting? It means they go to a special place, usually a school or a hall. They get a paper, and on that paper are the names of different people who want to be the leader. They put a special mark next to the name of the person they think will be the best leader. Why do people vote? Because it's their way to tell everyone who they want to lead them. It's like saying, 'I choose this person to help make our country better!' It's a very important way for people to have a say in their country's future. Each vote is like a little voice. Did you know? In many African countries, people also vote to choose their leaders! It’s a very common and important way for people to choose who governs them and make their voices heard.

Why Are People Voting in Hungary?

So, in Hungary, people are going to vote to decide something very important. They will choose if Mr. Viktor Orban, who has been the leader for 16 years, should continue to be the leader. Or, they might choose someone new. Many other people also want to be the leader! So, the people of Hungary will look at all the different choices. They will listen to what each person says they will do for the country. Then, they will vote for the person they think has the best ideas to help Hungary. It's a big moment for Hungary. It's when everyone gets to decide if they want to keep going the same way, or if they want to try a new way with a new leader. It's a way to plan for the future of their country.

What Does "Unseated" Mean?

The news article talks about whether Mr. Orban can be 'unseated.' This is a funny word, isn't it? It doesn't mean someone is going to push him off a chair! No, not at all. It's a special word for leaders. 'Unseated' means that if most people vote for someone else, then Mr. Orban would no longer be the main leader. The new person who gets the most votes would become the leader instead. He would step down from his very big job. It's a very peaceful way for a country to change its leaders. Instead of fighting, people just use their votes to decide. This is a very good thing because it means everyone's voice can be heard safely and fairly. Did you know? When a leader is 'unseated' by votes, it shows that the country believes in fairness and that everyone gets a chance to choose who leads them. This is called democracy, and it is a wonderful thing!

Nine Days Until the Big Day!

The special election day in Hungary is happening in just nine days! That's like counting from today until next week, and then two more days after that! It's coming very, very soon. Everyone in Hungary is getting ready. In these nine days, people will be talking, thinking, and getting ready to make their big choice. When the day comes, they will go to their voting places. After everyone has voted, all the little marks on the papers will be counted very carefully. It takes some time to count all the votes, like counting many, many beans! After all the counting, they will announce who the people of Hungary have chosen to be their leader. It will be a very exciting day for everyone there! The whole country will be listening for the news.

Why This is Exciting News!

So, why is this important for us to know, even though it's far away from Africa? Well, it teaches us something very important about how countries work. It shows us that people in many parts of the world get to choose who leads them. It shows that change can happen peacefully. It's about people using their voices, through voting, to build the kind of country they want to live in. This is a powerful lesson! It's always good to learn about other countries and how they make big decisions. We are all connected in this big, beautiful world! And it’s fun to learn about other cultures and how they do things. Perhaps one day, you might even visit Hungary and see the castles and taste the paprika!
summary: In Hungary, a country far away, people are getting ready to vote in nine days to decide if Mr. Viktor Orban, who has been their leader for a very long 16 years, should stay or if they should choose someone new.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how people in countries can choose their leaders peacefully through voting, and how leaders can change to bring new ideas and a fresh start for their country.
